name: Southern Baptist Church of New York
description_short: Southern Baptist Church of New York Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative established in 1978, serving over 100 members with savings accounts, loans, and personal banking services.
description_long: Southern Baptist Church of New York Federal Credit Union was established in 1978 and is headquartered in New York, New York. As a federally chartered credit union, it operates as a not-for-profit institution focused on serving members of religious groups, particularly those affiliated with the Southern Baptist Church community. The credit union offers a range of financial products including regular savings accounts, share draft accounts, money market accounts, share certificates (CDs), auto loans, personal loans, and personal banking services. Members can access online banking, mobile app services for iOS and Android, online bill pay, and financial calculators. The institution also provides mortgage options and advertises competitive interest rates typical of not-for-profit credit unions. Southern Baptist Church of New York distinguishes itself through its religious community focus and alignment of services with spiritual values. As a credit union, it emphasizes member ownership and reinvestment of profits back to members through better rates and lower fees compared to for-profit banks. The organization maintains a single branch location at 12 West 108th Street in New York and currently operates with over 2 dedicated employees. However, this is an extremely small institution with only $86,400 in assets and 100+ members, which may limit service capacity, product depth, and technological sophistication compared to larger credit unions or banks. The institution's tiny asset base raises questions about long-term viability and whether it can adequately serve member needs or weather financial stress. Prospective members should carefully consider whether such a small institution can provide the services and stability they require.
pros: Member-owned not-for-profit structure with profits reinvested into lower fees and better rates
Specialized focus on religious community members, aligning financial services with spiritual values
Offers multiple account types including savings, money market, and share certificates
Provides both auto and personal loans for member financial needs
Online banking and mobile app available for iOS and Android devices
Online bill pay capability for member convenience
Established institution with 46+ years of operating history since 1978
cons: Extremely small institution with only $86,400 in assets and 100+ members, raising sustainability concerns
Only 1 branch location and 0 ATMs, severely limiting physical access and convenience
Restricted membership eligibility limited to religious group members, not open to general public
Very small workforce (2+ employees) may limit customer service capacity and responsiveness
Limited product information on website suggests potentially narrower service offerings than larger credit unions
best_for: Members of the Southern Baptist Church community seeking relationship-based banking
Religious individuals wanting financial services aligned with spiritual community values
People in the New York area already connected to this specific church congregation
diagnosis: This credit union is best suited for active members of the Southern Baptist Church of New York who prioritize community alignment over convenience and product breadth. The critical caveat is the institution's micro-scale ($86K in assets, 100 members, 1 branch, 2 employees), which presents significant risks around service reliability, product availability, and long-term sustainability that prospective members must carefully weigh.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
